pi_items.source: Confirmed as valid Linux user directory path (ga user home directory); setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/LCA_Results directory at line 21-22

pi_items.verified_value: Plausible USLCI process name for corrugated packaging manufacturing
pi_items.source: Web search found multiple LCA studies on 'corrugated board boxes' as a packaging product. USLCI includes paper and packaging products (550+ processes documented). Export script searches for regex 'corrugated*box*' at line 70, indicating this name exists in target database.

Verified as Western Electricity Coordinating Council (WECC), one of six NERC regional entities managing bulk electric system reliability in Western Interconnection. Confirmed at Wikipedia and WECC.org. USLCI eGrid datasets confirmed to include WECC region electricity data (search: 'USLCI eGrid WECC region')

pi_items.verified_value: Plausible USLCI process name for kraft paper/linerboard manufacturing
pi_items.source: Linerboard is confirmed as real manufacturing process (kraft paper production). USLCI includes paper products. Export script searches for 'linerboard' at line 70. However, exact process name in USLCI database not directly confirmed in public documentation.

Verified as Southeastern Electric Reliability Corporation (SERC Reliability Corporation), one of six NERC regional entities. Confirmed at Wikipedia, FERC, and NERC sources. SERC covers southeastern and central US states. USLCI eGrid datasets confirmed to include regional electricity data.

100 bytes is reasonable minimum for CSV export of LCIA results. A typical CSV header line (e.g., 'Impact Category,Value\n') plus one data row easily exceeds 100 bytes. openLCA supports CSV export of LCIA results (ask.openlca.org/2862).

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real data sources: USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database from NREL) and TRACI 2.1 LCIA methods (EPA). WECC (Western Electricity Coordinating Council) and SERC (Southeastern Electric Reliability Corporation) are verified as real NERC regional entities with electricity grid coverage in the US. USLCI includes over 550 processes covering paper, manufacturing, and regional electricity mixes via eGrid datasets. The specific process names 'Corrugated board boxes' and 'Linerboard' are plausible for USLCI (which documents paper products and packaging) and match the search patterns in the export verification script, but exact naming in the USLCI database was not independently confirmed. openLCA can export LCIA results to CSV format. The 100-byte CSV minimum size requirement is reasonable for a results file.
